testing_centers.notes: In-person test centers worldwide and online proctored via OnVUE

id_requirements: Two forms of ID are required. Primary ID must be government-issued, include candidate's name, photo, and signature (e.g. passport, driver's license). Secondary ID must include candidate's name and signature or name and photo.
exam_rules: No notes, scratch paper, electronic devices, books, or study materials permitted. Personal items must be stored. No food or drink except water. Restroom breaks allowed but timer continues. Testing center staff may monitor via video/audio.
renewal_options: Earn CE credits through CompTIA CE program (training, conferences, publishing, teaching, higher certs) or retake the current exam version before expiry.
target_audience: Professionals with 2–3 years of experience in database administration
recommended_experience: 2–3 years of experience in a database administrator role recommended

prerequisites.description: 2–3 years of experience in a database administrator role recommended

retake_policy.notes: No waiting period required before first retake. After the second failed attempt, candidates must wait 14 calendar days before any subsequent attempt. No limit on total attempts.

objectives.title: Database structure types
sub_objectives: Comparing relational and non-relational databases like NoSQL, DynamoDB, and MongoDB

objectives.title: SQL code development
sub_objectives: Applying data definition language (DDL), data manipulation language (DML), transaction control language (TCL), ACID (atomicity, consistency, isolation, durability) principles, stored procedures, and views

objectives.title: Scripting methods
sub_objectives: Comparing server-side vs. client-side scripting and using languages like Python, PowerShell, and command-line scripting

objectives.title: Programming impact
sub_objectives: Using object-relational mapping (ORM) tools like Hibernate and Entity Framework, validating SQL code, and analyzing database server impact

objectives.title: Planning and design
sub_objectives: Gathering requirements, designing database architecture, and creating documentation like data dictionaries and entity relationship diagrams (ERDs)

objectives.title: Implementation and testing
sub_objectives: Validating schemas, performing stress tests, managing version control, and testing database connectivity

objectives.title: Deployment phases
sub_objectives: Installing, configuring, provisioning, and validating scalability

objectives.title: Monitoring and reporting
sub_objectives: Tracking system alerts, performance metrics, transaction logs, and resource utilization

objectives.title: Maintenance processes
sub_objectives: Optimizing queries, managing patches, planning capacity, and tuning performance

objectives.title: Documentation and tools
sub_objectives: Using data dictionaries, ERDs, standard operating procedures (SOPs), and compliance documentation

objectives.title: Data management tasks
sub_objectives: Modifying data, managing redundancy, defining relationships, and using views/materialized views

objectives.title: Data security concepts
sub_objectives: Applying encryption (in transit, at rest), data masking, and destruction techniques

objectives.title: Governance and compliance
sub_objectives: Implementing data loss prevention, retention policies, and regulations like the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)

objectives.title: Authentication and authorization
sub_objectives: Managing access controls, password policies, and identity management

objectives.title: Infrastructure security
sub_objectives: Applying physical controls like biometrics and fire suppression, and logical controls like firewalls and port security

objectives.title: Threat mitigation
sub_objectives: Addressing SQL injection, denial of service (DoS), phishing, ransomware, and brute-force attacks

objectives.title: Backup and recovery
sub_objectives: Creating disaster recovery plans, replication strategies, and failover processes

objectives.title: High availability
sub_objectives: Implementing clustering, load balancing, and system redundancy

objectives.title: Risk assessment
sub_objectives: Conducting impact analysis, risk mitigation, and continuity testing

official_study_resources.title: CertMaster Learn
official_study_resources.notes: Official e-learning with performance-based questions and study guides

official_study_resources.title: CertMaster Practice
official_study_resources.notes: Adaptive practice question engine with rationale
